candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: B has lower resource use, higher waste, improved habitat and more species. A has biological decline, C has weak biological gain, and D has rising resource use with flat biology. Correct B handles the trade-off with biological evidence. (b) Distractor types: A is Type II resource-only with negative biology, C is Type I no-trade-off shortcut, D is Type III waste-only shortcut with rising resource pressure. (c) Cognitive dimensions: trade-off reasoning, resource-waste comparison, habitat interpretation, species response and distractor elimination. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests weighing resource and waste effects, not choosing one metric. (e) Knowledge link map: resource benefit plus waste cost plus biological outcome. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: no row wins all measures and D no longer offers equivalent biological support; labels are neutral.

shortcut_check.reviewerNote: Pure text format is allowed here because the stem contains compact scenario or numeric data; the answer requires multiple inferences.

findings: B is uniquely defensible because it combines lower resource use with clear biological improvement while preserving the intended waste trade-off.
A has biological decline, C lacks biological gain despite better resource and waste values, and D has rising resource pressure with flat biology.
The item has no missing-data cue, no mapping-noise shortcut, equal option lengths, four QKL/KLM links, and closed write/runtime flags.
